evals.prompt: I just finished adding a new login page with OAuth support. Can you create a PR for this branch?
evals.expected_output: A PR is created with a conventional commit title (feat: ...), a body with Summary and Test plan sections, and the PR URL is returned.
expectations: PR title starts with 'feat:' since this is a new feature
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period
PR body contains a '## Summary' section with bullet points
PR body contains a '## Test plan' section with checkbox items
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool: gh CLI if available, mcp__github__create_pull_request MCP tool if not

evals.prompt: open a pr. i fixed the bug where the sidebar was overlapping the main content on mobile screens
evals.expected_output: A PR is created with a fix: prefix title, body includes Summary and Test plan, and the PR URL is returned.
expectations: PR title starts with 'fix:' since this is a bug fix
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period
PR body contains a '## Summary' section with bullet points
PR body contains a '## Test plan' section with checkbox items
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool: gh CLI if available, mcp__github__create_pull_request MCP tool if not

evals.prompt: Submit a PR for the dependency updates I just did — bumped react from 18 to 19 and updated the lockfile
evals.expected_output: A PR is created with a chore: prefix title about dependency updates, body includes Summary and Test plan, and the PR URL is returned.
expectations: PR title starts with 'chore:' since this is a dependency update
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period
PR body contains a '## Summary' section with bullet points
PR body contains a '## Test plan' section with checkbox items
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool: gh CLI if available, mcp__github__create_pull_request MCP tool if not
